I completely understand your frustration—hitting a roadblock with matrices can be incredibly annoying, especially when you are otherwise feeling highly confident in your computer science skills. 

**CS Learning Tip:** 
Try visualizing matrices as **2D arrays** or grids in code. Instead of seeing abstract math symbols, think of a matrix as an image (where each element is a pixel coordinate) or a game board. Mapping matrix operations (like transposition or multiplication) to nested loops in a programming language you know can make the concepts feel much more concrete.

**Encouraging Next Step:**
